title: Tangy Velvety Chinese Polish Sausage Buddha Jumps Over Wall
description: Tangy, velvety, energizing Chinese dish featuring Polish sausage, shio-cure, flexitarian-friendly, inspired by the mythical martial art move and ancient Chinese legend of the Monkey King.

ingredients.name: Buddha's hand citron
ingredients.notes: available at Asian markets or online

instructions: Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add Polish sausage and cook, breaking up with a spoon, until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add shio-cured pork belly and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy, about 5 minutes.
Add sliced mushrooms and stir-fry until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 3 minutes.
Add diced bamboo shoots and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add chopped scallions and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add sliced Buddha's hand citron and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Season with soy sauce and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped scallions and sliced citron.
For a vegan version, replace Polish sausage with extra-firm tofu, drained and cut into small pieces, and omit the shio-cured pork belly and soy sauce.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce and check the ingredients of the Buddha's hand citron for gluten content.
For a paleo version, replace Polish sausage with grass-fed beef, omit the shio-cured pork belly and soy sauce, and serve with cauliflower rice instead of steamed rice.
